question.content: In the diode-circuit set, use panel (b) with diode drop UD=0.7 V to determine output voltage Uo2. Output exactly one decimal number with 2 decimal places, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to two decimal places; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Decide the conduction state of each diode in panel (b) and solve node voltages.
step_1.key_point: Determine conducting diodes and solve node voltages in case (b).

step_3.content: Analyze panel 1 of 5 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_3.key_point: Establish the first intermediate relation for Uo2.

step_5.content: Analyze panel 2 of 5 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_5.key_point: Refine the relation to isolate Uo2.

step_7.content: Analyze panel 3 of 5 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_7.key_point: Substitute diode-drop constraints into the Uo2 relation.

step_9.content: Analyze panel 4 of 5 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_9.key_point: Obtain the final intermediate formula used to compute Uo2.

step_11.content: Compute Uo2 from the conducting diode path and format to two decimals.
step_11.key_point: Uo2 from the conducting diode path.
